verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 (also known as TAPL) is a lysosomal/late-endosomal ABC transporter (half-transporter) that localizes predominantly to lysosomes and late endosomes, where it transports peptides into the lysosomal lumen. It is a multi-pass TM protein but faces the lysosomal lumen, not the extracellular space. Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented stress/activation-induced PM translocation; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no evidence of surface display in any lineage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — while lysosomes can undergo exocytosis, there is no gene-specific evidence (flow cytometry, surface biotinylation, surface proteomics on intact cells) that ABCB9 reaches the PM via this route; (4) dual_localization — no PM pool documented; (5) stable_surface_attachment — ABCB9 is not a secreted protein. No therapeutic antibody/ADC program targeting ABCB9 on the cell surface is documented. Verdict: endomembrane-resident lysosomal transporter, not accessible from the extracellular face.
